message:
fix: add runtime validation and defensive coding to brenner-loop

1. confidence.ts: Add runtime validation for discriminativePower (1-5)
   - Imports isDiscriminativePower from evidence.ts
   - Throws clear error if invalid value passed at runtime
   - Prevents undefined in explanation strings from STAR_RATINGS lookup

2. storage.ts: Add optional chaining in createSummary
   - session.hypothesisCards?.[...] prevents TypeError on malformed sessions
   - Enables recoverSessions to recover sessions with missing hypothesisCards

3. misc.test.ts: Add test for invalid discriminativePower validation

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"brenner_bot-0s7","title":"Legacy: Build excerpt builder component (superseded)","description":"# Task

Create the excerpt builder that allows users to select text from documents and build cited excerpt blocks.

# User Flow

1. User browses a document (e.g., transcript)
2. User selects text
3. Selection popover appears: 'Add to excerpt'
4. Selection added to 'Excerpt Basket' (sidebar or drawer)
5. User can add multiple selections
6. User composes final excerpt with ordering and context
7. Export as formatted markdown for session kickoff

# Excerpt Basket

- List of selections
- Each shows: text snippet, source doc, §-anchor
- Drag to reorder
- Delete button
- 'Add note' for each selection

# Output Format

```markdown
## Context for Brenner Loop

### From §42: The choice of organism
> The choice of the experimental object remains one of the most important things...

### From §87: Abundance trick
> If you can find a protein that dominates...

### User note
[Additional context or question]
```

# Persistence

- Store selections in localStorage
- Clear on 'Start Session' or manual clear

# Integration

- Works within document viewer
- Selections persist across page navigations
- Basket accessible from any page

# Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Text selection triggers popover
- [ ] Selections added to basket
- [ ] Basket shows all selections with metadata
- [ ] Reordering works
- [ ] Export generates correct format
- [ ] Persists across navigation","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:15:35.813553979-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T20:15:59.636868573-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T19:29:11.486732227-05:00","close_reason":"Superseded by brenner_bot-5so.4.2.2 + brenner_bot-5so.2.4.1","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-0s7","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-v2e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:16:25.083902194-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-0xzg","title":"Task: Coverage Threshold Enforcement (80%)","description":"# Goal
Raise test coverage thresholds to 80% and enforce per-file minimum coverage.

# Current State
```typescript
// vitest.config.ts (current)
coverage: {
  thresholds: {
    lines: 50,
    functions: 50,
    branches: 50,
    statements: 50,
  }
}
```

# Target State
```typescript
// vitest.config.ts (target)
coverage: {
  provider: 'v8',
  reporter: ['text', 'lcov', 'html', 'json'],
  reportsDirectory: './coverage',
  include: ['src/**/*.ts', 'src/**/*.tsx'],
  exclude: [
    'src/**/*.test.ts',
    'src/**/*.test.tsx',
    'src/__fixtures__/**',
    'src/types/**',
    '**/*.d.ts',
  ],
  thresholds: {
    // Global thresholds
    lines: 80,
    functions: 80,
    branches: 75,
    statements: 80,
    
    // Per-file enforcement
    perFile: true,
    
    // Critical files with higher thresholds
    'src/lib/artifact-merge.ts': {
      lines: 90,
      functions: 90,
      branches: 85,
    },
    'src/lib/delta-parser.ts': {
      lines: 90,
      functions: 90,
      branches: 85,
    },
  },
  
  // Fail CI on threshold violation
  skipFull: false,
  
  // Check for uncovered lines
  checkCoverage: true,
}
```

# CI Integration
Update GitHub Actions workflow:
```yaml
# .github/workflows/test.yml
- name: Run tests with coverage
  run: bun test --coverage
  
- name: Check coverage thresholds
  run: |
    # Parse coverage report and fail if below threshold
    bun run coverage:check
    
- name: Upload coverage to Codecov
  uses: codecov/codecov-action@v3
  with:
    files: ./apps/web/coverage/lcov.info
    fail_ci_if_error: true
```

# Coverage Badge
Add to README:
```markdown
[![Coverage](https://codecov.io/gh/user/brenner_bot/branch/main/graph/badge.svg)](https://codecov.io/gh/user/brenner_bot)
```

# Gradual Rollout Plan
1. Week 1: Set global to 60%, add perFile
2. Week 2: Raise to 70%, identify low-coverage files
3. Week 3: Raise to 80%, enforce on all files
4. Ongoing: Add per-file exceptions only with justification

# Coverage Exclusion Guidelines
Exclude from coverage (with comment justification):
- Type definitions (*.d.ts)
- Test files and fixtures
- Generated code
- Configuration files
- Development-only code

DO NOT exclude:
- Complex business logic
- Error handling paths
- Edge cases

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Global threshold at 80% lines
- [ ] Per-file enforcement enabled
- [ ] Critical files at 90%
- [ ] CI fails on threshold violation
- [ ] Coverage badge in README
- [ ] Coverage report uploaded to Codecov
- [ ] Exclusion list documented","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T17:54:32.108038217-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-31T13:53:40.25802117-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-31T13:53:40.25802117-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","labels":["testing infrastructure coverage ci"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-0xzg","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-0meb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T17:56:20.596496889-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-0xzg","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-ponv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T17:56:20.62687603-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-0xzg","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-4ep3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T22:37:16.960318921-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"brenner_bot-17wm","title":"Fresh-eyes audit: artifact-merge.ts deterministic merge logic","description":"# Goal
Fresh-eyes audit of the artifact merge logic (apps/web/src/lib/artifact-merge.ts), which is the core deterministic delta compilation engine.

This file is ~2,800 lines of critical protocol logic that:
- Parses structured deltas from agent messages
- Merges ADD/EDIT/KILL operations deterministically
- Validates artifact integrity (references, lifecycles)
- Produces canonical markdown output

## Scope
- Identify logic bugs, edge cases, or error handling gaps
- Check for potential race conditions or order-dependent bugs
- Review validation logic completeness
- Verify tests cover critical paths
- Add focused tests for any gaps found
- Fix obvious bugs (no deletions per AGENTS.md)

## Complements brenner_bot-ukzy
GrayBear is doing a broader audit across CLI, Agent Mail client, API routes, etc. This is a focused deep-dive on one critical file.

## Acceptance
- All identified bugs documented or fixed
- Test coverage verified/improved for artifact merge logic","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonValley","created_at":"2026-01-01T20:46:18.588046977-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-01T20:49:59.533221275-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-01T20:49:59.533221275-05:00","close_reason":"Audit complete. No critical bugs found. Code is well-structured with proper security (prototype pollution protection), determinism (timestamp sorting, deep cloning), idempotency (KILL ops), and comprehensive test coverage (108 tests passing). Shared findings with GrayBear and OrangePuma."}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-1cv2","title":"Tutorial UX: Mobile Experience & Limitations","description":"# Purpose

Document and handle the reality that tutorial steps requiring terminal commands cannot be fully completed on mobile devices. Ensure mobile users have a good experience anyway.

---

# The Reality

Many tutorial steps require:
- Running terminal commands (git, bun, ./brenner)
- Copying multi-line code blocks
- Running AI agents (Claude Code, Codex)

**These cannot be done on mobile.** A phone or tablet user physically cannot:
- Run a terminal
- Clone a repo
- Execute CLI commands

---

# Design Decisions

## Option A: Gate terminal steps to desktop (CHOSEN)

Show a clear message on terminal-required steps when on mobile:
\`\`\`
📱 This step requires a desktop computer

The commands below need to be run in a terminal on your 
Mac, Windows (WSL), or Linux computer.

You can:
• Read through this step to understand what's involved
• Bookmark this page and return on desktop
• Continue to the next step (some content is mobile-friendly)
\`\`\`

## Option B: Mobile-optimized read-only mode

Automatically switch to "read-only" mode on mobile:
- All content visible
- Code blocks show but note "run on desktop"
- Progress tracking still works
- Can complete "understanding" checkboxes, not "execution" checkboxes

---

# Implementation

## 1. Device Detection
Use user-agent or viewport width to detect mobile.

## 2. Step Metadata
Add \`requiresDesktop: boolean\` to step data:
\`\`\`typescript
interface TutorialStep {
  // ...existing fields
  requiresDesktop?: boolean;
  mobileAlternative?: string; // What to show on mobile instead
}
\`\`\`

## 3. Mobile Message Component
\`\`\`tsx
function MobileDesktopGate({ children, step }: Props) {
  const isMobile = useIsMobile();
  
  if (isMobile && step.requiresDesktop) {
    return <MobileNotice step={step} />;
  }
  
  return children;
}
\`\`\`

## 4. Which Steps Need Desktop?

**Quick Start**:
- Step 2 (Prerequisites): Desktop required
- Step 3 (Clone & Install): Desktop required
- Step 4 (Search Corpus): Desktop required
- Step 5 (Build Excerpt): Desktop required
- Step 6 (First Session): Desktop required
- Step 7 (Understand Output): Mobile OK (reading)

**Agent-Assisted**:
- Steps 2-7: Desktop required
- Step 1, 8: Mobile OK (reading/understanding)

**Multi-Agent**:
- All steps: Desktop required (infrastructure setup)

---

# Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Mobile detection works reliably
- [ ] Desktop-required steps show clear message on mobile
- [ ] Users can still read step content on mobile
- [ ] Progress tracking works on mobile (marks steps as "viewed")
- [ ] Desktop users see no change","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T15:34:28.987529053-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-04T15:08:48.244621638-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-1cv2","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-do3j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T15:35:25.632137409-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-1es5","title":"Spec: Experiment Result Classification and Interpretation","description":"# Goal
Define how experiment results are classified and interpreted into DELTAs.

# Two-Stage Process (Key Insight)

**Stage 1: Classification** (structured, can be assisted)
- What KIND of result is this?
- Pass/fail/error/metrics/observation/anomaly?

**Stage 2: Interpretation** (judgment, guidelines only)
- What does this mean for hypotheses?
- What DELTA should be created?

# Stage 1: Result Classification

## The Taxonomy (Restored)
```typescript
type ExperimentResultType = 
  | "pass"           // Test completed, outcome matches expectation
  | "fail"           // Test completed, outcome contradicts expectation
  | "error"          // Test couldn't complete (crash, timeout, missing deps)
  | "metrics"        // Quantitative measurements without clear pass/fail
  | "observation"    // Qualitative finding requiring interpretation
  | "anomaly";       // Unexpected result that doesn't fit any prediction

interface ClassifiedResult {
  experiment_id: string;
  test_id: string;
  
  // Classification
  result_type: ExperimentResultType;
  confidence: "high" | "medium" | "low";
  
  // Extracted data (type-specific)
  metrics?: Record<string, number>;
  observation?: string;
  error_message?: string;
  
  // Which hypotheses this relates to
  hypothesis_refs: string[];  // ["H1", "H2"]
}
```

## Classification CLI (Assistant, Not Automator)
```bash
# Suggest classification based on output
./brenner.ts experiment classify \
  --session RS-20251230-bio-rrp \
  --experiment exp-a1b2c3

# Output:
# Result type: metrics (confidence: high)
# Extracted metrics:
#   bleu_vanilla: 0.42
#   bleu_rrp: 0.48
#   bleu_freq_penalty: 0.45
# 
# Related hypotheses: H1, H2 (based on test T1 definition)
# 
# Suggested interpretation pattern: "metrics_comparison"
# See: ./brenner.ts experiment interpret --pattern metrics_comparison
```

# Stage 2: Interpretation Patterns

Classification tells you WHAT you have. Interpretation decides WHAT IT MEANS.

## Pattern: pass → Predictions Update
**When**: Test passed, result matches hypothesis prediction
**DELTA**:
```json
{
  "operation": "EDIT",
  "section": "predictions_table",
  "target_id": "P1",
  "payload": {
    "actual_H1": "Confirmed: 0.03 BLEU difference, within equivalence threshold"
  },
  "rationale": "T1 passed: equivalence holds at tested scale"
}
```

## Pattern: fail → Hypothesis Weakening
**When**: Test clearly contradicts a hypothesis
**DELTA**:
```json
{
  "operation": "EDIT",
  "section": "hypothesis_slate",
  "target_id": "H1",
  "payload": {
    "status": "weakened",
    "evidence_against": "T1 showed 15% quality gap at 16k context"
  },
  "rationale": "H1 predicted equivalence; T1 found significant divergence"
}
```

## Pattern: error → Test Blocked
**When**: Experiment couldn't complete
**DELTA**:
```json
{
  "operation": "EDIT",
  "section": "discriminative_tests",
  "target_id": "T1",
  "payload": {
    "status": "blocked",
    "blocker": "OOM at 16k context, need 32GB instance"
  },
  "rationale": "T1 could not complete due to resource constraints"
}
```

## Pattern: metrics → Attach Raw Data
**When**: Have numbers but interpretation unclear
**DELTA**:
```json
{
  "operation": "EDIT",
  "section": "discriminative_tests",
  "target_id": "T1",
  "payload": {
    "actual_results": {
      "bleu_vanilla": 0.42,
      "bleu_rrp": 0.48,
      "bleu_freq_penalty": 0.45
    },
    "status": "awaiting_interpretation"
  },
  "rationale": "T1 produced metrics. Interpretation TBD."
}
```

## Pattern: anomaly → Register for Investigation
**When**: Result doesn't match ANY prediction
**DELTA**:
```json
{
  "operation": "ADD",
  "section": "anomaly_register",
  "payload": {
    "name": "Latency spike at 4k boundary",
    "observation": "RRP showed 3x latency at exactly 4096 tokens",
    "conflicts_with": ["H1", "H2"],
    "status": "active"
  },
  "rationale": "No hypothesis predicted latency effects"
}
```

## Pattern: observation → Critique or Assumption
**When**: Qualitative insight from running experiment
**DELTA** (if it challenges framing):
```json
{
  "operation": "ADD",
  "section": "adversarial_critique",
  "payload": {
    "name": "Metric choice confound",
    "attack": "BLEU may not capture the coherence benefits RRP provides",
    "evidence": "Running T1 revealed outputs differed in structure, not token overlap",
    "current_status": "Observed during experiment, needs investigation"
  },
  "rationale": "T1 execution revealed potential metric confound"
}
```

# When Patterns Don't Fit
- If result is ambiguous between patterns → discuss in thread
- If multiple patterns apply → create multiple DELTAs
- If no pattern fits → add to anomaly_register with status: "unclassified"

# CLI Helper
```bash
# Show interpretation guidance for a pattern
./brenner.ts experiment interpret --pattern anomaly

# Interactive mode: walk through classification and interpretation
./brenner.ts experiment interpret --interactive \
  --session RS-20251230-bio-rrp \
  --experiment exp-a1b2c3
```

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] ExperimentResultType taxonomy defined
- [ ] ClassifiedResult schema defined
- [ ] experiment classify command implemented
- [ ] All interpretation patterns documented in specs/
- [ ] experiment interpret helper command
- [ ] Guidance on when to ask for help

# Relationship to Other Work
- Depends on: Execution protocol (brenner_bot-t62l)
- Uses: Existing delta-parser and artifact-merge
- Enables: Artifact evolution from experiment evidence","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-30T18:51:12.453215707-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-31T00:21:04.888114637-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-31T00:21:04.888114637-05:00","close_reason":"All 4 child tasks completed: ExperimentResult schema (1es5.1), artifact types (1es5.2), experiment encode CLI (1es5.3), golden fixtures + tests (1es5.4)","labels":["infrastructure","protocol","research-loop"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-1es5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-t62l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T18:52:25.940633427-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-1es5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.12","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T18:59:54.950352935-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"brenner_bot-bom","title":"Audit: deep code review + hardening pass","description":"# Goal
Do a repo-wide, first-principles review of recently landed web/CLI/installer work (from multiple agents) to catch obvious bugs, security issues, reliability problems, and protocol mismatches.

# Scope (initial)
- Installer surface: `install.sh` + toolchain manifest handling (pinning, checksum verification, shell safety).
- Agent Mail integration: `brenner.ts` client + `apps/web/src/lib/agentMail.ts` + API routes using it.
- Session/orchestration logic: status parsing, kickoff composition, delta parsing/merge, auth/gating for side-effectful routes.

# Approach
- Run broad static scans (UBS) and targeted tests.
- Trace flows across imports/callers and check invariants.
- For each issue: diagnose root cause → minimal fix → add regression test when appropriate.

# Done when
- High-confidence issues found are fixed, tested, committed, and pushed.
- Any remaining risky items are filed as follow-up beads with crisp repro steps.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T02:07:02.358977148-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T02:50:41.856988742-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T02:50:41.856988742-05:00","close_reason":"Audit pass landed: session KICKOFF subject normalization + legacy kickoff parsing, mobile transcript deep-link/restore fix (69w), UBS scan run (0 critical in apps/web/src/lib), and release workflow env inlining + auth test cleanup.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-bom","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-30T02:07:02.384431182-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"brenner_bot-bzl","title":"Test: Jargon component unit tests","description":"# Jargon Component Tests

## Goal
Write comprehensive unit tests for the Jargon component following project's NO MOCKS policy.

## Test Categories

### 1. Rendering Tests
- Known term renders with dotted underline
- Unknown term renders children without styling
- Custom children override default term display
- className prop is applied

### 2. Tooltip Behavior (desktop mock)
- Hover triggers tooltip visibility
- Mouse leave hides tooltip (after delay)
- Focus triggers tooltip visibility
- Blur hides tooltip (after delay)
- Tooltip content includes term, short, analogy

### 3. Sheet Behavior (mobile mock)
- Click/tap opens sheet
- Backdrop click closes sheet
- X button closes sheet
- Sheet content includes full term details

### 4. Accessibility
- Button has proper aria-label
- aria-expanded reflects state
- Focus management is correct

### 5. Edge Cases
- Term key normalization (spaces → hyphens, lowercase)
- Portal renders to document.body
- Cleanup on unmount (no memory leaks)

## Test Setup

```typescript
// Use real dictionary data
import { jargonDictionary, getJargon } from "@/lib/jargon";

// Mock matchMedia for mobile detection
beforeEach(() => {
  Object.defineProperty(window, 'matchMedia', {
    value: vi.fn().mockImplementation(query => ({
      matches: query === '(max-width: 768px)' ? isMobile : false,
      addEventListener: vi.fn(),
      removeEventListener: vi.fn(),
    })),
  });
});
```

## Files
- `apps/web/src/components/__tests__/jargon.test.tsx`

 {"id":"brenner_bot-wcr8","title":"Tutorial Component: TutorialProgress (step indicator sidebar)","description":"# Component: TutorialProgress

The progress indicator that shows users where they are in the tutorial path.

## Behavior

**Desktop (width >= 768px)**:
- Vertical list on left sidebar
- Fixed position, scrolls with page
- Shows all steps with numbers
- Current step highlighted with primary accent
- Completed steps show checkmark + muted color
- Clickable to navigate (if allowed)

**Mobile (width < 768px)**:
- Horizontal progress bar at top
- Minimal text, mostly visual
- "Step X of Y" label
- Collapses to icon on scroll

## Props
\`\`\`typescript
interface TutorialProgressProps {
  steps: Array<{ id: string; title: string; estimatedTime: string }>;
  currentStep: number;
  completedSteps: number[];
  onStepClick?: (stepIndex: number) => void;
  allowJumpAhead?: boolean;
}
\`\`\`

## Tests
- Renders all steps
- Highlights current step
- Shows checkmarks for completed
- Click navigation works when allowed
- Mobile breakpoint layout","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T14:54:53.679913239-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-04T13:34:09.443698194-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T13:34:09.443698194-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented: TutorialProgress.tsx exists with 29KB of code including desktop sidebar, mobile dots, swipe gestures, animations, and all specified features","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-wcr8","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-thzu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T14:55:41.743176235-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"brenner_bot-wpb","title":"Create OperatorSymbol component with tooltip","description":"# Task

Create a distinctive inline component for Brenner operator symbols.

# The Operators

⊘ Level-split | 𝓛 Recode | ≡ Invariant-extract | ✂ Exclusion-test
⟂ Object-transpose | ↑ Amplify | ⊕ Cross-domain | ◊ Paradox-hunt
ΔE Exception-quarantine | ∿ Dephase | † Theory-kill | ⌂ Materialize
🔧 DIY | ⊞ Scale-check

# Design

Each operator symbol should:
1. Stand out from surrounding text (monospace font, slightly larger)
2. Have a subtle background pill
3. Show tooltip on hover with name + definition
4. Link to the operator palette page

# Visual

```
The ⊘ Level-split operator separates...
    ^
    └── pill background, hover shows tooltip
```

# Tooltip Content

- Operator name
- One-line definition
- 'Learn more →' link

# Props

```tsx
interface OperatorSymbolProps {
  symbol: '⊘' | '𝓛' | '≡' | '✂' | '⟂' | '↑' | '⊕' | '◊' | 'ΔE' | '∿' | '†' | '⌂' | '🔧' | '⊞';
  showName?: boolean;  // Show name alongside symbol
  linkToPage?: boolean; // Make it a link to operator page
}
```

# Operator Data

Create a data file with all operator definitions:
```typescript
export const OPERATORS = {
  '⊘': { name: 'Level-split', definition: 'Separate program from interpreter...' },
  // ...
};
```

# Animation

- Subtle scale on hover
- Tooltip fade in

# Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All 14 operators render correctly
- [ ] Tooltips show on hover
- [ ] Links work if enabled
- [ ] Accessible (keyboard tooltip trigger)
- [ ] Works inline with text","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:22:13.719833091-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T19:29:11.392954222-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T19:29:11.392954222-05:00","close_reason":"Folded into brenner_bot-5so.4.5.2 (operator palette UI)","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-wpb","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-8yn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:23:06.878775514-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"brenner_bot-x0o2","title":"Task: Define Test Record and Execution Schemas","description":"# Task: Define Test Record and Execution Schemas

## Objective
Create TypeScript types and Zod schemas for test records, predictions, and execution tracking.

## Implementation Details

### File Location
`apps/web/src/lib/test-binding.ts`

### Core Schemas
```typescript
import { z } from 'zod';

export const TestStatusSchema = z.enum([
  'designed',     // Test designed but not yet started
  'in_progress',  // Test is being executed
  'completed',    // Test has results
  'blocked',      // Test cannot proceed (resources, prerequisites)
]);

export const PredictionSchema = z.object({
  hypothesisId: z.string(),
  prediction: z.string().min(5),  // What this hypothesis predicts
  rationale: z.string().optional(),  // Why this prediction follows
});

export const TestRecordSchema = z.object({
  id: z.string().regex(/^T-[A-Z0-9]+-\d{3}$/),
  description: z.string().min(10),
  targetHypotheses: z.array(PredictionSchema).min(1),
  designedInSession: z.string(),
  designedBy: z.string().optional(),
  status: TestStatusSchema,
  methodology: z.string().optional(),  // How to execute the test
  resources: z.string().optional(),  // What's needed
  execution: z.lazy(() => TestExecutionSchema).optional(),
  createdAt: z.string().datetime(),
  updatedAt: z.string().datetime(),
});

export const ConfidenceSchema = z.enum(['high', 'medium', 'low']);

export const TestExecutionSchema = z.object({
  executedAt: z.string().datetime(),
  executedBy: z.string().optional(),
  result: z.string().min(5),  // The raw observation/finding
  matchedPredictions: z.array(z.string()),  // hypothesis IDs
  violatedPredictions: z.array(z.string()),  // hypothesis IDs
  confidence: ConfidenceSchema,
  notes: z.string().optional(),
  artifacts: z.array(z.string()).optional(),  // Links to data, figures, etc.
});

export type TestRecord = z.infer<typeof TestRecordSchema>;
export type TestExecution = z.infer<typeof TestExecutionSchema>;
export type Prediction = z.infer<typeof PredictionSchema>;
export type TestStatus = z.infer<typeof TestStatusSchema>;
export type Confidence = z.infer<typeof ConfidenceSchema>;
```

### ID Generation
```typescript
export function generateTestId(sessionId: string, sequence: number): string {
  const paddedSeq = sequence.toString().padStart(3, '0');
  return `T-${sessionId}-${paddedSeq}`;
}
```

### Validation
```typescript
export function validateTestRecord(data: unknown): ValidationResult {
  const result = TestRecordSchema.safeParse(data);
  if (result.success) {
    return { valid: true, errors: [] };
  }
  return {
    valid: false,
    errors: result.error.errors.map(e => `${e.path.join('.')}: ${e.message}`),
  };
}

export function validatePredictionConsistency(test: TestRecord): ValidationResult {
  const errors: string[] = [];
  
  // Check that all target hypotheses exist
  // Check that predictions are mutually distinguishable
  // Check that at least 2 hypotheses are targeted (discriminative test)
  
  if (test.targetHypotheses.length < 2) {
    errors.push('Discriminative test should target at least 2 hypotheses');
  }
  
  return { valid: errors.length === 0, errors };
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All test-related types defined with Zod
- [ ] ID generation follows pattern
- [ ] Prediction schema links to hypotheses
- [ ] Execution schema captures results and interpretation
- [ ] Validation ensures discriminative test design

## Dependencies
- Feature: Hypothesis Registry Schema (for hypothesis ID format)

## Effort Estimate
~150 lines of code, ~1.5 hours","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:46:53.766561476-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-31T00:46:13.955680805-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-31T00:46:13.955680805-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented in apps/web/src/lib/schemas/test-record.ts and prediction.ts with comprehensive tests","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-x0o2","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-m6yw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:49:01.97414199-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-x0o2","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-lm3b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:49:02.159569393-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-x0o2","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-jcuz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T22:41:23.734488729-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
